skill.name: brewpage-publish
skill.description: Publish content to brewpage.app — text, markdown, JSON, file, or multi-file site. Asks namespace and password, returns public URL. Triggers: publish, share link, upload to brewpage, host page, brewpage, publish site, upload site, upload directory, deploy site, сделай публичную ссылку, опубликуй.
skill.summary: Instantly publish text, files, or entire sites to brewpage.app and get a public URL with optional password protection.
skill.author: kochetkov-ma

security_audit.summary: The skill is a legitimate publishing tool for brewpage.app. All network requests target the documented brewpage.app API endpoints. External commands use standard tools (curl, jq, zip, mktemp) for content upload. The skill implements security safeguards: it excludes .env, .git, node_modules, and other sensitive paths from site uploads, never prints owner tokens in conversation, and stores them in a private file. The static scanner flagged many false positives because the skill's core function inherently requires network access and command execution. No malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: Network requests to external service
low_findings.description: The skill makes HTTPS requests to brewpage.app API endpoints to publish content. This is the core functionality of the skill and is explicitly documented.

low_findings.title: External command execution for content publishing
low_findings.description: The skill executes bash commands (curl, jq, zip, mktemp) to publish content and manage temporary files. Commands are hardcoded with no user input injection vectors.

low_findings.title: Filesystem access for content upload
low_findings.description: The skill reads user-specified files and directories for upload, and creates temporary zip files in /tmp. It excludes sensitive paths (.env, .git, node_modules) from site archives as a security safeguard.

dangerous_patterns.title: Owner token storage in local file
dangerous_patterns.description: Owner tokens for published pages are stored in .claude/brewpage-history.md. The skill explicitly warns to keep this file out of version control and never share it.

content.value_statement: Sharing files or sites usually requires setting up hosting, accounts, and configuration. This skill publishes text, files, and entire sites to brewpage.app with a single command, returning a public URL ready to share.
actual_capabilities: Publishes text and markdown to brewpage.app as rendered HTML pages
Uploads JSON documents to public or password-protected URLs
Uploads individual files (images, PDFs, binaries) with preserved MIME types
Zips and publishes entire static site directories as multi-file sites
Generates human-readable namespace slugs from content context
Stores owner tokens securely in a local history file for later deletion
limitations: Only publishes to brewpage.app — cannot use custom hosting providers
Maximum TTL is 30 days — content is temporary by design
Site uploads require built static output (dist/, build/, etc.) — not project sources
Site URLs cannot be verified with curl alone — require a real browser

use_cases.title: Share a quick report with a teammate
use_cases.description: A developer needs to share a markdown report with a colleague. They invoke the skill, get a public URL, and send the link — no email attachments or file transfers needed.

use_cases.title: Deploy a static site for review
use_cases.description: A designer finishes a static site build and wants client feedback. They point the skill at the dist/ directory, set a password for privacy, and share the URL.

use_cases.title: Host a JSON API mock or data file
use_cases.description: A frontend developer needs a public JSON endpoint for testing. They publish a JSON file to brewpage.app and use the URL as a mock API in their frontend code.

prompt_templates.title: Publish simple text content
prompt_templates.prompt: Publish "Hello, world! This is my first brewpage post." to brewpage.app

prompt_templates.title: Publish a markdown file
prompt_templates.prompt: Publish ./README.md to brewpage.app with a 7-day TTL

prompt_templates.title: Deploy a built static site
prompt_templates.prompt: Publish my ./dist directory as a site to brewpage.app, use namespace "my-project" and password-protect it

prompt_templates.title: Upload a file with custom TTL
prompt_templates.prompt: Upload ./screenshot.png to brewpage.app, set TTL to 1 day, namespace "screenshots"

output_examples.output:
Published: https://brewpage.app/public/abc123
Owner token saved to .claude/brewpage-history.md

Published site: https://brewpage.app/public/xyz789
Entry: index.html | Files: 12
Owner token saved to .claude/brewpage-history.md

best_practices: Always point site uploads at built output directories (dist/, build/, out/) — never at project sources
Keep .claude/brewpage-history.md out of version control — it contains owner tokens for deletion
Use password protection when publishing drafts or previews that should not appear in the public gallery
anti_patterns: Do not publish raw project source trees with package.json or src/ — the skill will warn or fail to protect you from leaking source code
Do not share owner tokens in conversation or commit them to version control — they allow deletion of your published content
Do not append a trailing slash to site URLs — this routes to brewpage.app's landing page and breaks the link

faq.question: What is brewpage.app?
faq.answer: brewpage.app is a free instant hosting service for HTML pages, JSON documents, files, and multi-file sites. It requires no sign-up and content expires after a configurable TTL.

faq.question: How long does published content stay available?
faq.answer: Default TTL is 15 days, with a maximum of 30 days. Use the --ttl flag to set a custom duration in days.

faq.question: Can I delete content after publishing?
faq.answer: Yes. The owner token is saved to .claude/brewpage-history.md. Use it with the DELETE API endpoint shown in that file to remove your content.

faq.question: Why does the skill exclude .env and .git from site uploads?
faq.answer: These exclusions are a security safeguard. They prevent credentials, secrets, and version control history from being accidentally included in your public archive.

faq.question: Can I publish to a service other than brewpage.app?
faq.answer: No. This skill is specifically designed for brewpage.app's API. For other hosting providers, you would need a different skill or tool.

faq.question: What happens if my site URL has a trailing slash?
faq.answer: brewpage.app's no-slash URLs use an inline JavaScript redirect that only executes in a real browser. Appending a trailing slash bypasses this redirect and routes to the landing page instead of your site.
